questions.question: What is a graph in the context of computer science?
questions.explanation: A graph is a non-linear data structure consisting of nodes or vertices connected by edges. It can be used to represent complex relationships between data.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a directed graph?

options.B: A complete graph with an equal number of edges and vertices
options.D: A graph where edges represent one-way relationships

questions.explanation: A directed graph, also known as a digraph, is a type of graph where edges have direction and can represent one-way relationships between nodes.

questions.question: What is a subgraph?

options.A: A smaller graph derived from a larger graph by removing nodes
options.B: A complete copy of a larger graph
options.C: A smaller graph derived from a larger graph by removing edges
options.D: A new graph created independent of a parent graph

questions.explanation: A subgraph is a smaller graph derived from a larger graph by removing nodes without deleting their corresponding edges.

questions.question: What is the difference between an undirected graph and a directed graph?

options.B: The type of nodes in the graph

questions.explanation: The primary difference between undirected and directed graphs lies in the direction of the edges. Directed graphs have edges with direction, while undirected graphs do not.

questions.question: If a graph has 'n' number of nodes, how many possible edges will it have?
questions.explanation: In an undirected graph with 'n' nodes, each node can be connected to every other node, leading to (n-1) possible connections per node, which is (1/2)*n*(n-1) possible edges.

questions.question: What are the two main components of a graph data structure?
questions.explanation: The two primary components of a graph are edges and relationships, with nodes or vertices representing the points of connection between these relationships.

questions.question: Can a graph have multiple edges between the same pair of nodes?

options.A: Yes, but with a single edge value
options.B: No, it will be considered a single edge
options.C: Yes, but it will have a separate edge value for each edge
options.D: It depends on the graph type

questions.explanation: In most graph representations, it is possible for a graph to have multiple edges between the same pair of nodes, each with its own unique edge value.

questions.question: What is a path in a graph?

options.A: A collection of nodes connected by edges without repetition
options.B: A node or group of nodes with no connections
options.C: A subgraph that contains no nodes or edges
options.D: Any two connected nodes with at least one edge

questions.explanation: A path in a graph refers to a sequence of nodes along with the edges connecting them, without any repetition of nodes.

questions.question: What is the difference between a simple path and a cycle?

options.A: A simple path is a cycle and vice versa
options.B: A cycle has repeated edges while a simple path does not
options.C: A cycle has repeated nodes while a simple path does not
options.D: A cycle has two or more edges and repeated nodes while a simple path has no repeated nodes

questions.explanation: A cycle and a simple path differ in the presence of repeated nodes and edges. A simple path has no repeated nodes, whereas a cycle can have repeated nodes and two or more edges.

questions.question: If we have a graph with 'n' nodes, and each node is connected to every other node, how many edges will the graph have?
questions.explanation: In this scenario, each node will have (n-1) connections, but each connection is represented twice. Thus, the total number of edges will be n*(n-1)/2.

questions.question: What is a connected component?

options.A: A subgraph with no nodes but has edges
options.C: The largest subgraph with no isolated nodes
options.D: A graph with at least one path

questions.explanation: A connected component refers to the largest subgraph that still contains a path between any two nodes within it.

questions.question: How many connected components does a graph with 'n' isolated nodes have?
questions.explanation: A graph with 'n' isolated nodes is essentially 'n' separate subgraphs, each containing one node. Therefore, it has 'n' connected components.

questions.question: What is the 'degree' of a node in a graph?

options.A: The total number of edges in the graph
options.B: The number of edges connected to a node
options.C: The number of distinct nodes in the graph
options.D: The maximum number of edges connecting two nodes

questions.explanation: The degree of a node is the total number of edges that are connected to it, providing insight into its connectivity within the graph.

questions.question: Is the given graph directed or undirected? (Given the image of a graph)

options.C: Cannot be determined based on the image

questions.explanation: Without visual information, I assume this is a typical question; an actual image would be required to provide a correct answer.

questions.question: What is an induced subgraph?

options.A: A subgraph derived by removing edges from a larger graph
options.B: A subgraph derived by removing nodes from a larger graph
options.C: A subgraph created without referencing the original graph
options.D: A subgraph with the same number of isolated nodes

questions.explanation: An induced subgraph is a subgraph formed by taking all the edges from the original graph that connect nodes of the subgraph's node set.
